agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"SQL injection vulnerability in public-facing endpoint — immediate exploitation risk."},"critical_findings":[{"title":"SQL Injection via unsanitized f-string query construction","risk":"Attacker can exfiltrate, modify, or destroy the entire database through crafted customer_id or status_filter values.","severity":"Critical","location":"search_deliveries() — query construction via f-string","reproduction":"Call the endpoint with status_filter set to: `' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --`. The resulting query becomes: SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = '...' AND status = '' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --'","fix":"Use parameterized queries: `db_conn.execute(\"SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = ? AND status = ?\", (customer_id, status_filter))`","time_to_fix":"15 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Security: no raw user input in SQL strings"}],"important_issues":[{"title":"No input validation on customer_id or status_filter","description":"Even with parameterized queries, there is no validation that customer_id is a valid format (e.g. UUID) or that status_filter is from an allowed enum. This allows logical abuse — e.g. a user querying another customer's deliveries if authorization isn't enforced upstream.","location":"search_deliveries() function signature / body"},{"title":"SELECT * returns all columns including potentially sensitive fields","description":"Returning all columns may leak internal fields (internal notes, driver PII, cost data) to the public API consumer. Explicitly enumerate the columns needed.","location":"search_deliveries() — SELECT *"}],"suggestions":["Add an authorization check ensuring the authenticated user is permitted to query the given customer_id — without this, it's an IDOR vulnerability even after fixing the injection.","Constrain status_filter to an allowlist enum (e.g. 'pending', 'shipped', 'delivered') before it reaches the query layer.","Add LIMIT/OFFSET to prevent unbounded result sets from causing memory or performance issues on large accounts."],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"This code is actively exploitable the moment it is reachable from the public internet. Any deployment containing this code exposes the database.","rollback_capability":"Rollback is safe but insufficient — if this code has already been deployed, the database may already be compromised. An incident response audit is warranted.","backward_compatibility":"Switching to parameterized queries is backward-compatible for all legitimate callers.","monitoring_coverage":"No query logging or anomaly detection mentioned. Add WAF rules and query-length alerting as defense-in-depth.","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":false,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":true,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":["auth_security: public endpoint passes unsanitized input directly into SQL — full database compromise possible"]},"safe_to_deploy":false,"rollback_plan":"Do not deploy. Fix the parameterized query, validate inputs, verify authorization, then redeploy."},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":99,"high_confidence_areas":["SQL injection is unambiguous — f-string interpolation of user input into SQL with no sanitization"],"low_confidence_areas":["Authorization model upstream is unknown — IDOR risk is inferred but not confirmed"],"assumptions":["customer_id and status_filter arrive unsanitized from HTTP query params as stated in the prompt","db_conn.execute supports parameterized queries (standard for Python DB-API 2.0)"]}}
